channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Fuel protests have Ireland's government facing possible no-confidence vote
item.description: The prime minister announced new tax cuts to try to end the crisis that began after the U.S.-Israel war on Iran led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The government could face a no-confidence vote over its response to the fuel protests.
item.content:encoded:
The prime minister announced new tax cuts to try to end the crisis that began after the U.S.-Israel war on Iran led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The government could face a no-confidence vote over its response to the fuel protests.

(Image credit: Peter Morrison)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Millions of people are pretending to be AI chatbots — for fun
item.description: Websites like youraislopbores.me have become playgrounds for people looking for light relief in a bot-heavy world.
item.content:encoded:
Websites like youraislopbores.me have become playgrounds for people looking for light relief in a bot-heavy world.

(Image credit: Screenshot by NPR)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Virginia joins a national effort to ensure only popular vote winners become president
item.description: With Virginia on board, the National Popular Vote Compact is now enacted in states worth 222 electoral votes. Here's what that means.
item.content:encoded:
With Virginia on board, the National Popular Vote Compact is now enacted in states worth 222 electoral votes. Here's what that means.

(Image credit: Stephanie Scarbrough)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez

item.title: Eric Swalwell will resign from Congress. And, Trump feuds with Pope Leo over Iran War
item.description: Eric Swalwell is resigning from Congress after multiple women accused him of sexual assault and misconduct. And, Trump is feuding with Pope Leo, calling him weak on crime for opposing the war.
item.content:encoded:
Eric Swalwell is resigning from Congress after multiple women accused him of sexual assault and misconduct. And, Trump is feuding with Pope Leo, calling him weak on crime for opposing the war.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Israel and Lebanon set to meet for first direct talks in more than 30 years
item.description: An official briefed on Israel's strategy for the talks described Tuesday's meeting as "preparatory" and aimed at laying out a framework for future negotiations.
item.content:encoded:
An official briefed on Israel's strategy for the talks described Tuesday's meeting as "preparatory" and aimed at laying out a framework for future negotiations.

(Image credit: Anwar Amro)
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: The labor economics of 'Alien' — and its lessons for inequality on Earth
item.description: Behind the acid blood and jump scares of the Alien franchise is an even more insidious horror: a single employer with unchecked power. How Weyland-Yutani helps explain monopsony — and the rise of inequality on Earth.
item.content:encoded: Behind the acid blood and jump scares of the Alien franchise is an even more insidious horror: a single employer with unchecked power. How Weyland-Yutani helps explain monopsony — and the rise of inequality on Earth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: In the brain, objects seen and imagined follow the same neural path
item.description: New evidence finds that sight and imagination rely on the same neurons and use the same neural code.
item.content:encoded:
New evidence finds that sight and imagination rely on the same neurons and use the same neural code.

(Image credit: Marco Bottigelli)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: 2 local TV giants merged. Then a court stepped in
item.description: Local TV giant Nexstar's $6.2 billion deal to acquire rival Tegna won speedy approval from Trump administration regulators. But it faces a tough challenge from a pair of antitrust lawsuits.
item.content:encoded:
Local TV giant Nexstar's $6.2 billion deal to acquire rival Tegna won speedy approval from Trump administration regulators. But it faces a tough challenge from a pair of antitrust lawsuits.

(Image credit: Brendan Carr)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Why Congress is fighting over a central tool of American surveillance
item.description: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act is responsible for a huge share of intel collected by the U.S. Lawmakers and civil liberties advocates are worried it enables warrantless spying on U.S. citizens.
item.content:encoded:
Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act is responsible for a huge share of intel collected by the U.S. Lawmakers and civil liberties advocates are worried it enables warrantless spying on U.S. citizens.

(Image credit: Paul J. Richards)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Reps. Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales are stepping down amid misconduct allegations, the U.S. and Iran are both blocking oil exports, Trump deletes controversial post amid row with pope.
item.content:encoded: Reps. Eric Swalwell and Tony Gonzales are stepping down amid misconduct allegations, the U.S. and Iran are both blocking oil exports, Trump deletes controversial post amid row with pope.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin
